case_title: KELLEY CARLIN VS. WRIGHT GARDNER LLC ET AL
cause_of_action: OTHER NON EXEMPT COMPLAINTS (/ complaint for damages)

docket_entries.description: LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT WRIGHT GARDNER LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE THE PUNITIVE DAMAGES ALLEGATIONS IN THE COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF KELLEY CARLIN IS DENIED. MS. CARLIN ADEQUATELY ALLEGES THAT WRIGHT GARDNER ENGAGED IN DESPICABLE CONDUCT CONSTITUTING UNLAWFUL DISCRIMINATION, RETALIATION, AND HARASSMENT THAT WAS COMMITTED AND/OR RATIFIED BY MR. HASCHKA, WHICH IS SUFFICIENT TO SUPPORT A PRAYER FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. FORMAL ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: SEAN KANE, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600, MARIA.TORREANOM@GMAIL.COM (302/HK)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT WRIGHT GARDNER LLC'S DEMURRER TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DISCRIMINATION, THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR RETALIATION AND THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR HARASSMENT IN THE COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF KELLEY CARLIN IS OVERRULED AS TO ALL THREE CAUSES OF ACTION. MS. CARLIN ADEQUATELY ALLEGED THAT SHE WAS SUBJECT TO UNFAVORABLE DISPARATE TREATMENT BASED ON HER GENDER, DISABILITY AND AGE INCLUDING THE TERMINATION OF HER EMPLOYMENT, WHICH IS SUFFICIENT FOR A FEHA DISCRIMINATION CLAIM. MS. CARLIN ADEQUATELY ALLEGED THAT HER EMPLOYMENT WAS TERMINATED BECAUSE SHE COMPLAINED TO MR. HASCHKA THAT SHE HAD BEEN UNFAVORABLY DISPARATELY TREATED DUE TO HER GENDER, WHICH IS SUFFICIENT FOR A FEHA RETALIATION CLAIM. MS. CARLIN ADEQUATELY ALLEGED THAT SHE WAS SUBJECTED TO REPEATED UNWELCOME INTOLERABLE BEHAVIOR BY MR. FIGUEROA THAT INCLUDED NUMEROUS DEROGATORY COMMENTS REFLECTING ON HER DISABILITY, WHICH IS SUFFICIENT TO ALLEGE SEVERE AND/OR PERVASIVE MISCONDUCT FOR A FEHA HARASSMENT CLAIM. FORMAL ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: SEAN KANE, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600, MARIA.TORREANOM@GMAIL.COM (302/HK)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-15-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Atty. ANDREA LOH, ESQ. of Loh Legal (Tel:415-617-5508) appearing for plaintiff Kelley Carlin;
Atty. NOAH M. WOO, ESQ. of Walsworth WFBM, LLP (Tel: 415-781-7072) appearing for defendant Wright Gardner, LLC.

The matter is argued and submitted by counsel for both sides. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendant Wright Gardner LLC's motion to strike the punitive damages allegations in the complaint filed by plaintiff Kelley Carlin is denied. Ms. Carlin adequately alleges that Wright Gardner engaged in despicable conduct constituting unlawful discrimination, retaliation, and harassment that was committed and/or ratified by Mr. Haschka, which is sufficient to support a prayer for punitive damages.

FORMAL ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Sean Kane, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600, maria.torreanom@gmail.com (302/HK)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-15-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Atty. ANDREA LOH, ESQ. of Loh Legal (Tel:415-617-5508) appearing for plaintiff Kelley Carlin;
Atty. NOAH M. WOO, ESQ. of Walsworth WFBM, LLP (Tel: 415-781-7072) appearing for defendant Wright Gardner, LLC.

The matter is argued and submitted by counsel for both sides. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendant Wright Gardner LLC's demurrer to the first cause of action for discrimination, the fifth cause of action for retaliation and the sixth cause of action for harassment in the complaint filed by plaintiff Kelley Carlin is overruled as to all three causes of action. Ms. Carlin adequately alleged that she was subject to unfavorable disparate treatment based on her gender, disability and age including the termination of her employment, which is sufficient for a FEHA discrimination claim. Ms. Carlin adequately alleged that her employment was terminated because she complained to Mr. Haschka that she had been unfavorably disparately treated due to her gender, which is sufficient for a FEHA retaliation claim. Ms. Carlin adequately alleged that she was subjected to repeated unwelcome intolerable behavior by Mr. Figueroa that included numerous derogatory comments reflecting on her disability, which is sufficient to allege severe and/or pervasive misconduct for a FEHA harassment claim.

FORMAL ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Sean Kane, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600, maria.torreanom@gmail.com (302/HK)
